feed.title: Free the People
feed.subtitle: We are a small, grassroots organization seeking to support the resistance against the oligarchy...

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Hegseth's Congressional hearing and DOJ weaponized against Comey (again). Supreme Court guts the Voting Rights Act and Trump tells ABC to fire Kimmel (again). DHS funded but no money for ICE and Trump regime paying companies to abandon green energy contracts.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Tariff refunds begin and Virginia joins Gerrymandering War. FBI/Justice Department workforce depleted and Russia uses oil profits from loosened sanctions to fund war in Ukraine. United States' illegal Caribbean killing spree continues.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Trump proves his threats empty and impact of Orbán's loss on the US. Trump family openly engages in corruption and House representatives resign in disgrace. US blockades Strait of Hormuz and Trump plots 2026 election takeover.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Minnesota sues Trump regime and Republicans face challenges in midterms. Trump promises future pardons to staff while anonymous bets allow insiders to profit from Iran war. Trump delivers disgraceful Easter message and tries to hide it.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Americans say 'No Kings' and Pam Bondi fired. Trump threatens to commit war crimes while Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff. Trump orders creation of national voter list, legal immigrants face deportations to unknown countries, and DOJ drops legitimate investigations to focus on immigration.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Another Republican seat flips to Democrats and Idaho refuses to give voter data to feds. Hegseth prays for violence and House rejects Senate DHS funding deal. Trump threatens Iran's civilian infrastructure and uses ICE as his private domestic army.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Counterterrorism director resigns over Iran war, Mullin's character questioned in confirmation hearing, and Anthropic sues Trump regime. Student loans are being transferred to Treasury Department and U.S. Mint authorized to produce Trump coin. Trump regime threatens to revoke press licenses if he doesn't approve of reporting and Trump looking forward to 'taking' Cuba. Join us for No Kings Day #3 on March 28.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Trump regime scraps plan to protect civilians then spends $11bn in first 6 days of war. U.S. bombs elementary school killing 160, mostly children, while Trump sons engage in blatant profiteering. Join No Kings Day #3 on March 28.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Kristi Noem out of DHS. Republicans sow chaos in Texas primaries and US boosts Russian oil sales. Trump regime's plan to steal 2026 elections revealed. More ICE lies surrounding shooting of Ruben Martinez.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Supreme Court rules Trump tariffs illegal. Undisclosed ICE-stapo murders uncovered. Trump sends U.S. into war with Iran.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Noem attempts to silence free speech. Democrats hold the wrong line on DHS funding. ICE turning empty warehouses into concentration camps.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: District judge orders U.S. to allow return of CECOT prisoners. Trump regime repeals emissions standards and Pam Bondi puts on childish display before House. CBP uses military laser to shoot down party balloon. What happened when USAID was cut?

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Minneapolis nominated for Nobel Peace Prize, Alex Pretti's killers identified, and Kristi Noem may be impeached. Kennedy Center closing under Trump's failed leadership. Trump regime is making plans to take control of 2026 elections. The real story behind Trump's tariff madness.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: Nationwide protests demand ICE out of our cities. Trump regime targets journalists to silence free press. FBI seizes 2020 voter records in Georgia. Another (partial) government shutdown begins.

entry.title: Week in Review
entry.summary: Recap of this week in the revolution: ICE is not welcome in Minnesota, nor in the United States. The Trump regime still refuses to release the Epstein Files in violation of the law. ICE-stapo murders another in the streets. Trump's dementia is getting worse.
